topic.what: Similarity-constrained adversarial attacks on non-stationary malware detection systems that evade both classifier and drift monitoring
topic.how: Generates targeted adversarial examples in classifier feature space augmented with KL, ℓ2, and MMD regularizers to minimize drift signals while achieving misclassification
topic.why_matters: Reveals that attackers can exploit drift monitoring blindspots; shows perturbation budget is dominant driver of ASR-drift tradeoff; provides framework for evaluating stealthiness beyond misclassification

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of malware samples misclassified as benign after perturbation
characteristics.vs_baseline: 3.10% baseline; ℓ2 constraint maintains ~3% ASR; large budget + MMD achieves 96%
characteristics.context: Budget δ is dominant driver; step size and iterations have secondary effect

characteristics.what_itasures: Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistic between clean and adversarial classifier output distributions
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline 0.0183; ℓ2 reduces to 0.0121; MMD increases to 0.0865
characteristics.context: Lower values indicate adversarial outputs closer to clean distribution; KS sensitive to all regularizers

characteristics.what_itasures: Population Stability Index between clean and adversarial output distributions
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline 0.0025; ℓ2 reduces to 0.0008; MMD increases to 0.0673
characteristics.context: PSI less sensitive than KS in low-budget regime; all values below 0.1 threshold

characteristics.what_itasures: Jensen-Shannon divergence between clean and adversarial output distributions
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline 0.0212; ℓ2 reduces to 0.0124; MMD increases to 0.1099
characteristics.context: Statistical metrics consistently detect MMD-regularized attacks; KL and ℓ2 reduce drift

characteristics.what_itasures: Classification accuracy on clean test data
characteristics.context: Feed-forward neural network with 2 hidden layers, batch norm, dropout, ℓ2 regularization

characteristics.what_itasures: Impact of budget δ on ASR and drift trade-off
characteristics.context: Larger budget expands feasible perturbation set, increasing both evasion and detectability

novelty: Formulation of similarity-constrained targeted feature-space attacks with KL, ℓ2, MMD regularizers
Drift evaluation protocol comparing clean vs adversarial using JSD, Hellinger, Wasserstein, output-MMD, KS, PSI
Characterization of evasion-detectability trade-offs across objectives, optimizers, and hyperparameters
Demonstration that perturbation budget dominates ASR and drift; KL/ℓ2 reduce drift without improving ASR
open_problems: Black-box and transfer attacks against drift monitoring
Practical deployment considerations and real-world evaluation
